utilityName: Orange County Utilities Cross Connection Control
searchAliases: orange county utilities registered backflow testers
testingFrequency: On the county program cycle for protected assemblies
dueBasis: Orange County runs a real cross-connection control section, accepts backflow test reports by email, registers testers, and keeps a public search path for Registered Backflow Testers and Irrigation Contractors.
coveredPropertyTypes: Protected residential areas with reclaimed water infrastructure
County utility customers with protected service connections
officialListLabel: Open the Orange County Utilities cross-connection page for tester search instructions
penalties: Orange County routes the program through a dedicated cross-connection section and keeps accepted reporting inside county tools and the county backflow email. Missing the right county workflow can keep the device outside accepted compliance.
sourceExcerpt: Orange County is a strong Florida county utility because it combines a live cross-connection section, registered tester search instructions, backflow test forms, and reclaimed-water-specific device rules.
verdictSummary: Orange County is a strong Florida county utility because it exposes the real county program, the registered tester search path, and reclaimed-water-specific device rules.
whoIsAffected: Orange County Utilities customers with cross-connection hazards, irrigation systems, reclaimed-water interconnects, and protected service connections.
residentialNotes: Residential demand is strongest in reclaimed-water neighborhoods because the county explicitly addresses those areas.
The county even changed who installs dual-check devices in those protected reclaimed-water zones.
commercialNotes: Commercial value is strong because the county publishes the manual, tester search instructions, and direct report-submission contact.
This is a serious county workflow, not a light educational page.

annualTesting.summary: Orange County runs a formal cross-connection section with backflow test forms, a manual, and county-side reporting, which makes annual or recurring testing a real county workflow.
highlights: The county publishes backflow tools and standards.
Registered Backflow Testers and Irrigation Contractors are searchable from the public page.
Backflow test reports route through the county backflow email and section staff.
workflowSteps: Confirm the assembly is inside the Orange County program.
Use a registered tester or irrigation contractor found through the county search path.
Submit the report through the county workflow.

irrigation.summary: Orange County is especially good for irrigation because reclaimed-water and potable-interconnect rules are built directly into the public county page.
highlights: Residential reclaimed-water areas have county-specific dual-check rules.
The county publishes both a reclaimed-water customer guide and irrigation practices support.
Registered irrigation contractors are part of the same public search path.
workflowSteps: Check whether the property is in a reclaimed-water neighborhood or interconnect situation.
Use the county's registered tester or irrigation-contractor path.
Follow the county standards and report-submission workflow.

fireLine.summary: Orange County is less fire-line-specific than West Palm, but the same county standards and tester registration workflow still govern larger protected service assemblies.
highlights: The county standards cover multiple assembly types.
The cross-connection manual is part of the public workflow.
Registered tester search is still the next step for larger protected services.
workflowSteps: Confirm the protected service class and required device type.
Use the county's registered tester search path.
Submit results through the county section.
Check whether the service is under Orange County's cross-connection section.
Use the public search instructions to find a registered tester or irrigation contractor.
Complete the test and any county-required forms.
Send the report through the county workflow so the assembly stays compliant.
failureHighlights: Orange County publishes registered tester search instructions on the main program page.
Reclaimed-water and irrigation are first-class parts of the county program.
The county manual and standards are public.

costBand.testingRange: Testing is market-priced, but Orange County narrows the path through registered tester and irrigation-contractor search.
costBand.repairRetestRange: Repair and retest cost rises on reclaimed-water and interconnect conditions.
costBand.pricingNotes: The main local value is county-process clarity and a strong irrigation/reclaimed-water angle.
